title: Key separation check silently disabled in production (passphrase mode)
description:
Scenario: Normal wallet_init flow (with passphrase, which is the non-hidden default). The nie-dfu key separation assertion only runs when no_passphrase=true. In the standard passphrase flow the check is completely skipped.

Maintenance consequence: CLAUDE.md §Key separation invariant says this is a 'runtime assertion.' A contributor reading the docs will assume the assertion is always active. When they later add a code path that touches both keys, they will not add their own check, trusting that wallet_init already enforced it. The comment in code says 'avoid a second passphrase prompt' which is a real UX concern, but the consequence is that the assertion documented as a security guarantee is only active in --no-passphrase (CI/test) mode.

The check should either: (a) run unconditionally and be documented as a one-time cost at init time, or (b) be removed from the code and the comments should say 'enforced by independent entropy, not runtime assertion in passphrase mode' so a future reader does not misunderstand the guarantee.
